name: HEALTHSTREAM INC
business: HealthStream provides SaaS-based workforce solutions for healthcare organizations, including learning, credentialing, and scheduling applications. Its hStream technology platform enables interoperability and extends AI capabilities. The company also serves nursing schools and individual healthcare professionals.
sellsTo: Healthcare organizations across the continuum of care

exposures.sentence: HealthStream provides primarily SaaS based applications for healthcare organizations all designed to improve business and clinical outcomes by supporting the people who deliver patient care.
Our scheduling solutions provide organizations with the tools to visualize and manage real-time clinical staff scheduling to enable them to optimize their workforce, reduce costs, and improve care.
As our use of AI technologies grows, we may face increased risks of or susceptibility to cyberattacks or security threats.

catalysts.event: Quarterly dividend increase to $0.035 per share
catalysts.sentence: Our Board approved a quarterly dividend at a rate of $0.035 per share, which represented an increase of 12.9% compared to the quarterly dividend per share in 2025 as set forth above. This quarterly dividend shall be payable on March 20, 2026 to the holders of record of all of the issued and outstanding shares of common stock as of the close of business on March 9, 2026.

catalysts.event: Completion of $10 million share repurchase program
catalysts.sentence: the Company continued to repurchase shares pursuant to this authorization during the first quarter of 2026, completing the program in January by repurchasing 222,978 additional shares valued at $5.0 million.

tamClaims.claim: spending in the healthcare industry reached over $5.3 trillion in 2024, or 18.0% of the U.S. gross domestic product.
tamClaims.sentence: spending in the healthcare industry reached over $5.3 trillion in 2024, or 18.0% of the U.S. gross domestic product.

tamClaims.claim: approximately 23 million professionals are employed in the healthcare and social assistance sector of the domestic economy
tamClaims.sentence: approximately 23 million professionals are employed in the healthcare and social assistance sector of the domestic economy

tamClaims.claim: approximately 5.8 million employed in acute-care hospitals
tamClaims.sentence: approximately 5.8 million employed in acute-care hospitals and, according to CMS, approximately 6.9 million employed in other healthcare organizations throughout the continuum of care
